items.title: Zymeworks adds lung disease drug, royalty deals in nearly $1B pick up of Theravance
items.snippet: Zymeworks announced a new plan to become a royalty-driven company last year, making Theravance a perfect match.

items.title: Ipsen makes up to $1.75B blood cancer bet with Kartos buyout
items.snippet: The star of Ipsen’s acquisition is an MDM2 blocker being proposed as an add-on therapy to ruxolitinib for myelofibrosis. The drug could be available to patients “as early as 2028,” according to Ipsen CEO David Loew.

items.title: Viridian debuts on market with FDA greenlight for thyroid eye disease drug
items.snippet: Viridian Therapeutics’ Lumvoa is the first FDA-approved treatment for thyroid eye disease that includes data for both active and chronic forms of the illness.

items.title: BridgeBio’s achondroplasia pill shows ‘compelling’ growth and body proportionality benefit
items.snippet: BridgeBio Pharma plans to file for approval in the third quarter. If granted, the oral drug could present a “highly differentiated” option over current achondroplasia therapies, according to Jefferies.

items.title: 4 ovarian cancer candidates to watch: GSK, Allarity and more
items.snippet: Recent approvals for Corcept Therapeutics and Merck have injected momentum into the space, where GSK, Allarity Therapeutics, OSE Immunotherapies and others are advancing their own candidates.

items.title: Capricor thrown for another loop as FDA sets adcomm for DMD cardiomyopathy therapy
items.snippet: The FDA plans to hold an advisory committee meeting to discuss Capricor Therapeutics’ application for deramiocel, which the agency rejected last July. The news surprised CEO Linda Marbán, who told BioSpace the FDA has not communicated any issues of concern with the company’s resubmitted application.

items.title: Biogen axes 'small number' of Apellis roles after $5.6B buy
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Replimune gets early August decision date for twice-rejected melanoma drug
items.snippet: Replimune’s resubmission for RP1 for melanoma comes after the departures of FDA leaders in place at the time of the drug’s first two rejections. The FDA expects to hold an advisory committee meeting in late July.

items.title: BIO 2026: Making biotech IPOs great again and the forgotten pharma antitrust review
items.snippet: Dealmaking across biopharma is shifting dramatically as the SEC rolls out new regulations to ease burdens on newly public companies and antitrust review is replaced by drug pricing as the policy concern du jour.

items.title: The hidden cost of FDA unpredictability
items.snippet: When the variance can’t be modeled, even disciplined biotech investors stop deploying. Here’s the cheapest fix for biotech’s investability problem.

items.title: Otsuka’s drug eases symptom burden, anxiety in ADHD as FDA verdict nears
items.snippet: The positive ADHD data for Otsuka Pharmaceutical’s centanafadine is good news in what has of late been a mixed bag for the neuropsychiatric space.

items.title: Moderna looks out to R&D ‘horizons’ as 2028 break-even goal looms
items.snippet: Jefferies expects Moderna to have around seven commercial products in the coming years which, along with a projected 10% revenue growth, could help the company break even in 2028.

items.title: Merck KGaA inks $11B Bio-Techne buyout to expand into cell therapy production
items.snippet: The company is “especially excited” about an immune cell therapy manufacturing technology included in the deal, a Merck KGaA executive said.

items.title: BIO 2026: Forget dual track—biopharma deals are more complex than ever
items.snippet: Dual and even triple or quadruple track processes have come roaring back in 2026 thanks to a glut of M&A that has refilled investors’ wallets. Big Pharma is being put on notice that time is critical if they want to acquire.

items.title: BIO 2026: FDA’s IND initiative is a step toward normalization after slipshod year
items.snippet: Policymaking at FDA has been anything but business as usual under the Trump administration, but former regulators cite the agency’s new investigational new drug pilot program as a sign of normalcy.

items.title: The Golden Triangle and the gaps: Rethinking UK biotech
items.snippet: In this episode of Denatured, as part of our series on the European life sciences investment ecosystem, you’ll hear from Hakan Goker, managing director at M Ventures and Maina Bhaman, partner at Sofinnova Partners. We explore the UK biotech ecosystem: from the Golden Triangle’s evolving role to the challenges of scali...

items.title: Revolution’s second RAS blocker doubles chemo response rate in pancreatic cancer
items.snippet: Encouraging data for combination regimens of Revolution Medicines’ zoldonrasib “reinforce the path to leadership in PDAC” for the biotech, according to Truist Securities.

items.title: Ionis secures market expansion for Tryngolza as FDA clears severe hypertriglyceridemia use
items.snippet: The approval of Ionis Pharmaceuticals’ Tryngolza for severe hypertriglyceridemia could spur “substantial growth” for the product, according to William Blair.

items.title: Gilead’s TROP-2 Trodelvy, Keytruda combo win first-line FDA nods for breast cancer
items.snippet: Gilead’s Trodelvy can now be used as a monotherapy and in combination with Merck's Keytruda to treat certain patients with triple-negative breast cancer.

items.title: FDA’s uniQure, REGENXBIO reversals could bolster other near-term rare disease applications
items.snippet: The FDA’s recently altered outlook on the evidence required for approval of rare disease drugs could have immediate benefits for companies including Skyhawk Therapeutics, Capricor Therapeutics and Biohaven.

items.title: BIO 2026: Cell and gene therapy sector must break the mold to thrive
items.snippet: If cell and gene therapy makers are going to achieve their mission to improve patients’ lives, the industry must come together to share information across stakeholders, from regulators to manufacturers to payers.

items.title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
items.snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.
